column1: # grep-tells.tsv — TIER 1 (flat-presence) rules for audit-swiftui-menus-commands (menu-01 … menu-10).
# severity: hard | warn | adv   (hard → runner exits 2). The runner greps each ERE over the target *.swift.
# This LOCATES only — the LLM agent READS each hit (SKILL.md step 3) and decides. Structural rules that
# grep cannot scope (keyboardShortcut-outside-.commands, standard-name CommandMenu) live in ast-grep/*.yml.

column4: HALLUCINATED: @FocusedDocument is not a real Apple symbol → custom FocusedValues key (@Entry var document: …) + @FocusedValue(\.document). Corroborate: swiftui-ctx lookup FocusedDocument exits 3.

a CommandMenu/CommandGroup is present — READ it: if its closure references a concrete @State/model directly instead of @FocusedValue, the command targets the wrong window or won't compile (menu-03). Also confirm placement (menu-02/05) and routing (menu-07).
CommandMenu titled like a STANDARD menu → makes a SECOND File/Edit/View/Window/Help menu. Use CommandGroup(after:/replacing: .placement) to extend Apple's menu (menu-02).

CommandGroup(after: .appInfo/.newItem) — adds a SECOND About/New beside Apple's default. To override use CommandGroup(replacing: .appInfo/.newItem); to remove use replacing: with an empty closure (menu-05).
hand-rolled Help/Sidebar/Toolbar menu → use SidebarCommands() / ToolbarCommands() / CommandGroup(replacing: .help). Hand-rolling loses ordering, the Help search field, and localization (menu-06).

keyboardShortcut present — READ it: if on a Button/control that is NOT inside .commands { } and expected to be app-global, it fires only while focused and renders nothing in the menu (menu-04). Move the action into .commands.
focused-value routing present — confirm the command that consumes it carries .disabled(value == nil); without it the menu item fires against nothing or the wrong window (menu-07).
RESERVED-shortcut collision — never override ⌘Q (Quit) / ⌘H (Hide) / ⌘, (Settings) / ⌘Space (Spotlight). Pick another key (menu-09).

floored command API — .singleWindowList/commandsRemoved/commandsReplaced need macOS 13+, InspectorCommands() needs 14+, ImportFromDevicesCommands() needs 12+. REPORT only if deployment target is below that floor (read in ORIENT); gate #available(macOS 13/14, *) (menu-10).

commands-presence probe — if the WHOLE app has NO .commands{}/CommandMenu/CommandGroup/Commands AND app actions live as an in-window Button row, the menu bar is faked as buttons (menu-01). Confirm by reading the App body (a per-app, not per-line, judgment).
